{{Coord|38.023056|N|140.038889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|en|Kawanishi, Yamagata|WD=Q1349146|lt=Kawanishi, Yamagata|1=Kawanishi, Yamagata}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}; {{ill|Kawanishi|WD=Q1349146|1=Kawanishi, Yamagata}}| region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = burial tumulus | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access =yes | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Shimokomatsu Kofun group'''|下小松古墳群|Shimokomatsu kofun-gun}} is the collective name for an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} consisting of six separate clusters of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]] located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Kawanishi, Yamagata|en|Kawanishi, Yamagata|WD=Q1349146|lt=Kawanishi, Yamagata|1=Kawanishi, Yamagata}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2000.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/203416|title=下小松古墳群|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shimokomatsu Kofun group consists of 202 known ''[[kofun]]'' arranged into six clusters on a long ridge at an altitude of 230 to 280 meters in the Shimokomatsu neighborhood of Kawanishi. These include 21 {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} (which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above) or {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} ( "two conjoined rectangles" type keyhole tumuli). This cluster contains over half of the keyhole-shaped tumuli which have been found in Yamagata.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> These ''kofun'' were built from the end of the 4th century to the early 6th century AD. [[Archaeological excavation]]s are ongoing, and various {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, jewelry, weapons and agricultural tools have been recovered. The National Historic Site designation encompasses a total of 179 tumuli in three groups.
The site is located approximately 10 minutes by car from {{ill|Uzen-Komatsu Station|en|Uzen-Komatsu Station|WD=Q7904818|lt=Uzen-Komatsu Station|1=Uzen-Komatsu Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Yonesaka Line|en|Yonesaka Line|WD=Q1057090|lt=Yonesaka Line|1=Yonesaka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamagata)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamagata)|WD=Q9633915|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamagata)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Yamagata)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://yamagatakanko.com/spotdetail/?data_id=10464 Enjoy Yamagata home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.town.kawanishi.yamagata.jp/kyoiku/bunka/kofun.html Kawanishi home page official site] {{in lang|ja}}
